title: Add recursive fractal growth wireframe structures
summary: Create a cube-growth fractal generator inspired by nw_wrld's CubeGrowth technique. A seed cube spawns child cubes on its faces recursively (3-5 levels deep), with noise-driven branching for session uniqueness. All cube instances are merged into a single vertex buffer (Points) and a single edge-line buffer (LineSegments), following the US-065 wireframe pattern. The structure is audio-reactive via existing shader infrastructure.

new.purpose: Pure geometry generator: recursive cube-growth algorithm producing merged vertex and edge buffers
GeometrySystem wrapper: creates Three.js Points + LineSegments meshes from cubeGrowth output, wires audio-reactive shaders

modified.reason: Add 'fractalgrowth' case to extractSystemConfig; add maxFractalCubes budget field if needed
Add compound mode pairing (e.g. 'pointcloud+fractalgrowth') and register in primaryCountForSystem
Instantiate fractalGrowthWireframe system, add to singleEntries rotation and systemRegistry

steps.title: Implement cubeGrowth generator
steps.description: Create the recursive cube-growth geometry generator.
details: Define CUBE_VERTICES: 8 corner positions of a unit cube as a const array of [x,y,z] tuples
Define CUBE_EDGES: 12 index pairs referencing CUBE_VERTICES (the 12 edges of a cube)
Implement CubeGrowthConfig interface: { seed: string, depth: number, maxVertices: number, maxEdges: number }
Implement CubeGrowthOutput interface: { vertexPositions: Float32Array, edgePositions: Float32Array, vertexRandoms: Float32Array, edgeRandoms: Float32Array, vertexCount: number, edgeCount: number }

Implement generateCubeGrowth(config): recursive algorithm that starts from a seed cube at origin, then for each face of the current cube, uses seeded noise (createPRNG) to decide whether to spawn a child cube on that face (probability decreasing with depth)
Each cube instance is transformed by a 4x4 matrix: translate to face center + scale down by ~0.5-0.7 per level
Collect all cube instances' 8 vertices into a single merged vertex Float32Array (N*8*3 floats)
Collect all cube instances' 12 edges into a single merged edge Float32Array as LineSegments pairs (N*12*2*3 floats)
Generate per-vertex aRandom values using position-based deterministic hash (matching US-065 pattern)
Respect maxVertices and maxEdges budgets — stop recursing when budget is exhausted
Normalize final structure to TARGET_RADIUS (2.5) using the same normalizeToRadius pattern from fractalGeometry.ts
Validate all values are finite

steps.title: Implement fractalGrowthWireframe system
steps.description: Create the GeometrySystem that renders the cube-growth structure as Points + LineSegments.
details: Follow wireframePolyhedra.ts pattern exactly: import same shaders (noise3d.glsl, wireframe.vert.glsl, wireframe.frag.glsl, wireframeVertex.vert/frag)
Define FractalGrowthConfig interface: { maxFractalDepth?: number, noiseOctaves?: 1|2|3, enablePointerRepulsion?: boolean, enableSlowModulation?: boolean, maxEdgesPerShape?: number }
Implement createFractalGrowthWireframe(config) returning GeometrySystem & { cleanup(), setOpacity() }
In init(): call generateCubeGrowth with seed + ':fractalgrowth', depth from config (clamped 3-5), budget from maxEdgesPerShape
Create single BufferGeometry for edges with position + aRandom attributes → LineSegments mesh

Create single BufferGeometry for vertices with position + aRandom attributes → Points mesh (use extractUniqueVertices or use the vertex buffer directly since cube vertices are already unique per instance)
Use createEdgeUniforms / createVertexDotUniforms pattern from wireframePolyhedra.ts for ShaderMaterial setup
Additive blending, transparent, depthWrite: false
In draw(): update all shader uniforms from FrameState (uTime, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uMotionAmplitude, uPointerDisturbance, uPointerPos, palette, cadence, displacement, etc.)
In cleanup(): dispose geometry + material, remove from scene
In setOpacity(): update uOpacity uniform on both materials

steps.title: Register system in quality tier config
steps.description: Add extractSystemConfig case for the new system.
details: Add 'fractalgrowth' case to extractSystemConfig switch returning { maxFractalDepth, noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation, maxEdgesPerShape }
maxFractalDepth maps from profile.maxFractalDepth (already exists: low=3, medium=4, high=6, but clamp to 5 max for this system)
maxEdgesPerShape from profile.maxEdgesPerShape (low=30, medium=480, high=1920)
Add 'fractalgrowth' to primaryCountForSystem in compoundModes.ts returning maxEdgesPerShape

steps.title: Wire into main.ts rotation and registry
steps.description: Instantiate the system and add to mode rotation.
details: Import createFractalGrowthWireframe
Create instance with quality-derived config (same pattern as wireframe/cubeLattice)
Add SingleRotationEntry { kind: 'single', name: 'fractalgrowth', system, maxPoints: <edgeBudget> }
Add 'fractalgrowth' factory to systemRegistry for compound mode support
Optionally add a compound mode pairing in compoundModes.ts (e.g. 'pointcloud+fractalgrowth')

steps.title: Build verification and smoke test
steps.description: Verify the implementation meets Definition of Done.
details: Run npm run build — must pass with no errors
Load in browser — verify no console errors (especially no shader compile errors or WebGL validation errors)
Verify fractal structure renders as 3D wireframe with visible depth/parallax
Verify audio reactivity: bass drives macro displacement, treble drives fine jitter
Verify each session produces different growth patterns (refresh twice, compare)
Verify structure scales with quality tier (low tier = fewer cubes/edges, high = more)
Verify mute toggle and info overlay still work
Verify no localStorage/cookies/analytics used

architecture_notes.generator_vs_system_split: Generator (cubeGrowth.ts) is pure math — produces Float32Arrays with no Three.js dependency. System (fractalGrowthWireframe.ts) handles Three.js objects, shaders, and per-frame uniform updates. This matches the existing generators/ vs systems/ pattern.
architecture_notes.single_draw_call: All cube instances are merged into one vertex buffer and one edge buffer before creating geometry. This means one Points draw call and one LineSegments draw call total, not per-cube.
architecture_notes.cube_growth_algorithm: Start with seed cube at origin. For each of 6 faces, seeded RNG decides spawn probability (decreasing with depth). Child cube is positioned at parent face center, scaled down. Recurse. This produces organic tree-like structures of connected cubes.
architecture_notes.noise_driven_branching: Use createPRNG(seed + ':face:' + faceIndex + ':depth:' + d) for deterministic but unique branching per session. Branching probability ~0.6 at depth 1, decreasing by ~0.15 per level.
architecture_notes.budget_control: maxEdgesPerShape from quality tier controls total edge count. Generator tracks running count and stops spawning when budget is hit. Low tier: ~30 edges (2-3 cubes), Medium: ~480 (up to ~40 cubes), High: ~1920 (up to ~160 cubes).
architecture_notes.shader_reuse: Reuse existing wireframe.vert.glsl and wireframeVertex.vert.glsl shaders — they already handle bass/treble displacement via FBM noise. No new shader code needed.
